title: Doppler
description: Adds the famous Doppler effect into Minecraft's sound engine.
body:
The Doppler effect is a real-life sound phenomena that shows up as a **frequency** change when either you the listener or the source of the audio is moving, its better explained here: [Doppler Wiki](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Doppler_effect).

<hr>

**Compatible with [Simple Voice Chat](https://modrinth.com/plugin/simple-voice-chat) and [Plasmo Voice](https://modrinth.com/plugin/plasmo-voice).**

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/dTkfkTWfMPQ" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

### Config
Default effect "scale" is at 40%, noticeable but not too exaggerated, though I havent tested all means of movement next to audio sources, so you're free to change it in ModMenu's settings screen for the mod or in the config file located at `<minecraft>/config/doppler.cfg` (value is a decimal with ranges from 0-1 _(default: 0.4)_, though no hard limits).

![Location of modmenu's config button](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/8b6cc985ee9380276c13f4d9d5bb4caac629e686.png)

<hr>

### Why?
I kept seeing insta reels that have "doppler effect" caption with a clip of something in minecraft just decreasing in volume, which is NOT the effect in question!! 
So I made this mod, so you can hear what it really sounds like, who will use it? idk, but its fun 😸
